name: Merrill Lynch Financial Advisor Noreen Gillen
description_short: Merrill Lynch Wealth Management office on Park Avenue offering comprehensive financial advisory services, investment solutions, and banking products through Bank of America partnership.
description_long:
Merrill Lynch operates a wealth management office located at One Bryant Park on Park Avenue in New York, NY 10036, serving as part of the larger Merrill Lynch Wealth Management division. The office functions as a financial advisory hub connecting clients with multiple advisor groups specializing in personalized wealth management. The Park Ave office is currently temporarily closed, but operates as a full-service financial center during normal operations.

The office offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including personal banking, lending products, investment solutions, retirement and savings accounts, insurance products, and wealth planning services. Advisors at the location leverage both Merrill Lynch's investment resources and Bank of America's banking and estate planning services. The office houses multiple advisor groups, each operating as independent teams within the Merrill Lynch framework, including The Joachim Group, DiMattina Group, Kimber & Associates, and numerous others listed on their website.

What distinguishes this location is its emphasis on goal-oriented wealth management rather than transaction-focused advisory. The office positions itself as offering strategies developed from years of combined expertise, with advisors able to address concentrated stock positions, retirement income planning, and coordination with external professionals like accountants and tax attorneys. The integration with Bank of America provides additional depth in estate planning and lending products not available through advisory-only firms.

Clients should understand that Merrill Lynch advisors operate within a for-profit brokerage model and may have product incentives that could influence recommendations. The temporary closure status suggests potential operational changes. Prospective clients should verify current advisor availability and use FINRA's BrokerCheck to review individual advisor backgrounds and regulatory history, as emphasized on their website.
address: 1 Bryant Pk 5th Floor, New York, NY 10036

company_info.headquarters: 1 Bryant Pk 5th Floor, New York, NY 10036

pros: Access to both Merrill Lynch investment solutions and Bank of America banking products through single relationship
Multiple specialized advisor groups at one location, enabling choice among different team structures and specializations
Coordination with outside professionals (accountants, tax attorneys) explicitly offered as part of service model
Estate planning services available through Bank of America partnership
Concentration on goal-based planning rather than transaction volume
Established Park Avenue location in Manhattan's financial district
Direct access to FINRA BrokerCheck information for advisor verification
cons: Office is currently temporarily closed with no reopening date specified on website
As a for-profit brokerage, advisors may have compensation incentives tied to product sales
No pricing information or fee structure disclosed on website
Limited transparency about individual advisor qualifications or specializations within groups
No information provided about minimum account sizes or service requirements
best_for: High-net-worth individuals in New York seeking integrated banking and investment management
Business owners with concentrated stock positions requiring specialized guidance
Clients nearing retirement seeking income planning with estate planning coordination
diagnosis: This profile represents Merrill Lynch's Park Avenue wealth management office, best suited for affluent individuals and business owners seeking integrated financial services combining investment advisory with banking and estate planning. The critical caveat is that the office is currently temporarily closed, and prospective clients must verify current operations and advisor availability before initiating contact. Additionally, clients should independently review advisor credentials via FINRA BrokerCheck given the for-profit brokerage model's inherent compensation incentives.
services: Estate planning coordination with Bank of America
Access to planning tools and financial resources
Articles, insights, and market research (The BullPen)
